theme: compile one plan for many sparse matrix shapes
title: One Plan, Many Shapes: Cheapest Adaptive Probe Tree for a Sparsity Sweep
strategies: static plan: multiply the union of all nonzero blocks, no tests (trivial baseline)
probe-all: test every block, then multiply the ones that came back nonzero (greedy, the obvious adaptive recipe)

find the cheapest splitting invariant of the pattern family and build a shallow decision tree keyed on the discriminating blocks, so each pattern pays only its own multiplies plus a few identity tests (strong)
provably-optimal min-worst-case decision tree over the sweep with cross-branch multiply sharing (open ceiling, uncovered)
